title: anthyllis vulneraria f. valentina
intro: Anthyllis vulneraria f. valentina, also known as kidney vetch, is a plant species within the legume family, notable for its distinctive yellow flowers and traditional use in herbal medicine. The plant contains a range of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Kidney vetch has been employed to support kidney and urinary health, as well as to aid in the treatment of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation. The plant can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and dried extracts, which are often used to harness its health benefits.
